meaning: n.冒险,危险,冒险的事vt.冒...的危险,赌运气,使遭危险
examples: A new report suggests that chewing gum may be a health hazard.
Catching colds is unfortunately an occupational hazard in this profession.
The recent outbreaks of cholera are a timely reminder that this disease is still a serious health hazard.
The expensive gas grill she said was a fire hazard and dangerous to have around grandchildren.
We can only hazard guesses as to her motives but we may never find out.
